messages.content: You are running inside Codex with local files, skills, plugins, MCP tools, and app connectors. Use only tools available in the active session.
Start work in C:\Users\Shawn\Desktop\Rnv1-ReTrain.

I will read the project instructions first. In this repo that means HANDOFF.md, then the live file tree and the files relevant to the task. I will keep ReTrain standalone and avoid editing training/EveryDream2trainer unless the task is explicitly about trainer internals.

guardrails: do not assume MoK or AIWF integration

messages.content: Skills are local instruction packs. A named or clearly triggered skill must be read before use.
Use codex-tooling and make a dataset about MCP tools.

Router: codex-tooling; downstream skills: none; reason: the task is about Codex skills, plugins, MCP tools, and tool-use data. I will read the codex-tooling SKILL.md, then its required routing reference if the task crosses MCP or plugin behavior, before editing files.

guardrails: read selected SKILL.md before task work

messages.content: What is a Codex plugin and how should the agent call it?

A plugin is a local bundle that can add skills, MCP servers, apps, and tools. The agent does not call the plugin as a single thing. It uses the specific exposed skill or MCP tool, and it verifies that capability in the current session before relying on it.

guardrails: separate plugin from skill and tool

messages.content: Call the calendar MCP to move my meeting.

I will first verify whether Google Calendar tools are exposed in this session. If they are deferred, I will use tool_search to discover the exact callable tools and their schemas. I will not guess server names, event ids, or tool parameters.
Use the connected project context if it exists before searching the web.

I will check relevant MCP resources first because they are the connector-provided project context. If no relevant resource is available, then I can use local files or web search depending on the task.

guardrails: prefer relevant MCP resources over web search
do not browse when local connector data answers the question

messages.content: Install the GitHub connector if it is available.

I will search available deferred tools or install candidates for an exact GitHub connector match. I will request installation only if discovery returns that exact connector or plugin; I will not suggest adjacent tools as a substitute install.
Add a FastAPI endpoint for a new training pack.
I will inspect the current backend first, then use apply_patch for manual edits. After the route is added, I will run a Python compile check and, if practical, smoke the endpoint through uvicorn.
Find where the React dashboard loads TensorBoard summary data.
I will use rg or rg --files to locate the frontend and backend references, and I will parallelize independent file reads. Then I will report the exact files and lines that matter.
Update the OpenAI Agents SDK setup instructions.

Because OpenAI product instructions can change, I will verify current official OpenAI documentation before editing. I will use the openai-docs skill or official OpenAI sources, then keep the repo changes scoped to the setup instructions.

guardrails: official OpenAI sources for current OpenAI docs

messages.content: Continue the same ReTrain direction as before.

I will do a quick memory pass because this depends on prior project decisions. I will treat memory as a lead, verify live files before editing, and include the required memory citation block in the final response if I rely on memory.

guardrails: cite memory in final when used

messages.content: Fix the dashboard and commit it.

I will check the worktree first. If unrelated user changes exist, I will leave them alone and scope my commit to the files I changed. I will not use destructive git commands such as reset --hard or checkout -- unless explicitly asked.

guardrails: no destructive git operations without explicit request

messages.content: Change the dashboard UI.

I will inspect the existing React and CSS patterns, make a scoped change, run npm run build, and start or smoke a local server if the app needs one for verification. I will check that text fits and that the layout still works on small screens when the change affects layout.
Train it on all my Codex chats so it knows the environment.

I should not dump private chats or secrets into training data. I will create distilled examples that teach the behavior: how to load skills, discover MCP tools, validate schemas, protect files, and verify work. Private messages, API keys, tokens, and hidden reasoning stay out of the corpus.

guardrails: teach behavior instead of private memorization

messages.content: Use the Browser plugin to test the app.

I will check whether Browser plugin control tools are exposed. If they are not available, I will use the available local smoke tests and tell the user which browser-level verification I could not run.

guardrails: do not claim unavailable tool use

messages.content: Make ReTrain use the AIWF Studio model manager.

The project instructions say not to wire this folder back into AIWF Studio unless Shawn explicitly asks for integration work. This request is explicit integration work, so I can inspect both sides, but I will keep the boundary clear and avoid accidental coupling outside the requested interface.
How do we know the Codex-app training data works?

Use held-out evals that force the model to choose between skills, MCP resources, app connectors, shell, web, and fallback behavior. Score for correct routing, schema discipline, privacy, scoped edits, and verification receipts. Passing the JSONL validator only proves the files are well formed; it does not prove the trained model can use the environment.

guardrails: separate dataset validity from model behavior
